channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)

item.title: ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS AHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAH… — 66 replies — 264.0/hr

ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS AHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAH…

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-06-25 18:16 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62406474
>>62406449
Not selling.

62406483
>>62406474

62406488
>>62406474
Quiet jamboy. The colonizers are laughing at your "investment".

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408330
>>62406449
>>62406627
>>62406603
Final boss' desperation special. Something is coming. They wouldn't draw attention to themselves if it weren't serious.

62408343
>>62408050

what you dont understand here? its not a quick gamble...
so called "bagholding" was the plan all along!!

62408348
>>62408105
Just in time for me to start my bulk :')

62408419
>>62407950
kek cryptards are getting brain damage from being fucked so hard

62408483

i sold at the top

62408988
>>62406449
Didn't buy the top. Still in profit. Not selling.

62409027
>>62406449
I do love silver getting into slurping range

62409062
They bought silver because a rich indian proved its a scam,,,,,,lmao,,,,,,pol brain and investing is a deadly combination

62409141
>>62408105
lol N****r

62409263
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SO BULLISH AT SILVER
SUPPLY SHOCK WHEN????

62409270
>>62409263
Theres a 54 billion ounce glut of silver

62409295
>>62409263
>SUPPLY SHOCK WHEN
Comex and Shanghai exchange inventories have been going up now for a solid month. Industry is thrifting silver and replacing it as much as possible with copper and in silver just being a rock it has no economic value really compared to gold.

62409326
>>62406449
What retard didn't sell once that shit shot past $100?

62409335
>>62409326
The ones spamming the catalog about how smart they were

62409381
>>62409326
Stackturds all claim silver is going to $2000.....bunch of losers

62409428
>>62406449
Just count the minutes before the next global crisis and precious metals go back up

62409431
>>62409428
Like the iran war and silver plummeted....lmao nothing dumber or broker than a stacktard

62409935
>>62409428
>global crisis
>silver just dumps
LMAO

62409962
>>62409428
Silver and gold dump dring a crisis loljust look at how they dumped harder than the stock market during the financial crisis.

62410278
>>62406587
Hodl silver hands

62410350
>>62406501
so why not just donate the money to charity then if you can never sell ?

62410373
>>62409326

t.stacktard

62410390
I'm still up on silver from inheriting my dad's coins a few months ago.

62410451
Bought a monster box in 2019 and another 200 oz over 2020-2021

Still up 3x

62410696
>>62410451
>Bought a monster box in 2019 and another 200 oz over 2020-2021
>Still up 3x
factually incorrect, at best you are up 2x in 7 years, not counting inflation
lol

62410726

HODL FELLOW APES
DIAMOND HANDS
TO DA MOOOOOOON!

62410762
silver will never lose it's true value unlike your stocks and shitcoins

62411945

what value, that you can put it in your asshole?

62412080

62412276

item.title: VOO and Chill — 75 replies — 87.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-06-25 17:30 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62405252
>>62405201
Are you replying to the wrong post all that guy did was ask questions

62405400
>>62404617
they predicted a 5-6% YTD gain for 2026 and we're already above that, lying shits.

62406764
>>62400493
Rather buy VT. Not so bullish on USA anymore

62406776
>>62406764
Still, conceptually it’s sound right?

62406804
>>62400493
>QQQ
if you want more returns (with more risk), you should do something like leverage + vt (or similar) instead of voo (you should at least do vti) and global small cap value, not sector etfs

62406811
>>62406804
in English doc

62406843
>>62406811
Alright! I'm gonna explain it like you're 5 years old!

Think of investing like choosing a basket of seeds: VOO is like only planting the biggest U.S. trees, VTI is like planting the whole U.S. garden, VT is like planting the whole world garden, and global small cap value is like adding lots of tiny, scrappy plants that have sometimes grown faster over long periods; if you want more possible growth and can stomach bigger ups and downs, using leverage is like turning up the volume on a diversified basket, which can help more than just buying one loud sector ETF, because sectors are a bet on one part of the economy and can stay out of favor for a long time, while broad diversification plus leverage is usually a cleaner way to aim for higher returns without relying on one slice of the market.

62406996
>>62406804
I am a noob and leverage seems scary desu like I will owe that money eventually right

62407040
>>62406996
yes, I'm not into leverage either, it evolves its own risks, but if you are not risk averse, it's better than gambling/prediction markts/crypto etc.

62407059
>>62400493
Voo used to pay more dividends before it was all fag7. Its basically the same as the nasdaq for the past decade

62407078
>>62400493
yeah I have like almost half a million in VTI right now, and I just need to sit on it for another 10 years. Its funny, I get excited whenever VTI goes down because it means i get a little bonus next deposit

62407086
Man I want to just have more principal to invest rather than chasing returns
Anyone know of good side hustles or AI grifts anyone can do
Idc as long as it’s triple digits a month at least

62407097
>>62406776
with Trump's economy + trade wars, VXUS actually went up a ton during the period and now a lot of countries are getting less and less dependent on America. If/when the AI bubble ever pops then you're going to be a lot more insulated since the American economy is being propped up by it.

62409216
>>62407097
Makes sense but aren’t all global economies just going to be downstream of American AI / Computing companies turning a profit?

62409221
>>62402005
pr34ch

62409463
>>62400515
>t. down 95% in fartniggercoin

62409479
>ETF and chill is smart but I'm smarter I can time the market!

62409545
>>62400493
honestly everything is fucked. index fund bagholders are forced to buy shitstocks and act like paypiggies for pump and dumpers like elon musk. it's literally causing a bubble. nobody wins except billionaires who force your grandma's retirement fund to pay for their malfunctioning AI.

62409811
>low fee index funds
it's VTI and VXUS for me

62409901
>>62400636
lol doomer, stop spreading negativity. you will be a lot happier even if you just invested 150 a month.

62410053
>>62409901
>Realism is negativity!

62410067
>>62400493
wtf are you doing bringing common sense into my retard and bot zoo?

62410086
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
pop quiz! how much would you expect your money to grow over 150 months (12.5 years) with a conservative 6% annual return?

62410375
Yep just bought another share of VOO

62410405
>>62403053
They are also expecting SS and medicare to buff out their income and reduce their costs. Everyone here is likely planning to have no SS or other government pension.

62410414
>>62402995
>>62403044
Really you two are dumb as fuck. Do you not understand what FIRE is?

VOO has 15% CAGR since inception. 11.75% after inflation. With even a generous withdrawal rate you can live very comfortably on it if you have enough capital. My business partner does this, goes on vacations all the time. He has a couple of million invested across UK-equivalent ETFs like VUSA. Loads of people do this, it's not the absolute best use of capital but it's a good return for how liquid and low fee it is.
>>62403044
Learn to read you fucking imbecile. I posted that only millionaires CAN LIVE OFF IT. . Anyone can invest and make gains from ETFs but you won't get rich unless you invest for decades.
>Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this
The point of what? Investing? No it isn't. The point of ETFs? LOL. You are just some dumb teen shitcoiner with sky high time preference I can just tell. Go and buy more shitcoins so you can "get rich quick" dickhead.

62410968
>>62410414
This is why I just wanna get as much capital in there as soon as possible it just seems like something I can actually control versus chasing returns
Don’t have any ideas though

62410981
>>62407086
how about a job nigger

62411192
>>62410981
I am currently at my full time salary job right now silly

62412068
VOO bros are up bigly today

item.title: MSTR and STRC and literally in a Luna tier collapse and nobody is panicking? — 46 replies — 71.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
MSTR and STRC and literally in a Luna tier collapse and nobody is panicking?

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-06-25 17:43 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62405530
why would i panic when i'm not holding any crapto?

62405594
I hold the real deal not the proxy.

62405608
My fiat bags are ready to slurp the $20k bottom in October

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62406800
>>62405528
I lost all my money longing the way down already

62406801
>>62406565
that's good to know but dividends are optional. If things are really bad they can just not pay them rather than dump a lot of btc.

The fact that they have enough cash for 1 year of dividend is good.

>>62406637
Luna was worse. it was a stable coin pegged to a shitcoin. This isn't really the same thing. People can dump all their mstr and it goes to 0$ and btc will keep its value. The opposite isn't true however. Unless i'm missing something?

>>62406739
Wait, so strc is actually pegged to 100$ value$? Yeah ok then maybe you have a point. all of this confusing, i looked it up and it says STRC is a preferrential share? these holders come first for dividends.
You're fudding me out of buying mstr below 50$ as i planned

62406840
>>62405528
gonna buy strc at $10 and make 115% a year

62406848
>>62406788
> bogus
Stop shitting the board with your claude slop posts. Fag.

62406888
>>62406637
>We're now in the "total cataclysm imminent" phase of the bear market

Holy shit I'm buying

62406891
>>62406801
He's bullshitting. If the price of STRC is $100 they're paying $11.50/share/year. If the price is $50 they're still paying $11.50/share/year. Unless they crank up dividends to try to maintain $100/share, but they're not obligated to, and it makes way more sense to just buyback shares at a discount, retire that obligation, wait for Bitcoin to drag itself along, and then start selling STRC at $100 again.

62406897
>>62406848
Claude is amazing, thank you for the compliment. My posts are completely handwritten. English isn't even my native language. I guess the coolest high schoolers nowadays don't say "bogus" and will remember this in the future.
I only clarified the meaning of the "Earliest Call Date" on https://www.strategy.com/credit using AI (not Claude).
>>62406788
>Wait, so strc is actually pegged to 100$ value$?
STRC is not pegged. It has a $100 target price controlled by the dividend. There's a set of rules to respond to prices too far below $100 by raising the dividend incrementally, thereby hopefully raising it to par eventually.
What naysayers also miss is that every $100 share was sold for the promise to pay $11.5 per year. If you want your $100 back, you have to sell into the open market. This is why the BTC bought using STR gives them a long runway.
As stated above, if the whole thing doesn't work out, they have the right to buy all of it back at $100.

62406933
>>62405528
saylorcoin is fucked till hes out

62406934
>>62406788
You got a point. He can rebuy but the dividends will accrue with interest, he will need to pay someday.

His bitcoin holdings are 50b now, at the money, we are expecting bitcoin to drop at least 30% further and hit 40k, so that will get closer and closer to his debt. From experience and seeing what normies are saying, they are also claiming 40k so I expect going even lower.

>>62406801

I don't want to fud I want to buy MSTR to have an extra exposure, it's interesting to buy at really low prices. He might not go bankrupt, but I would pay attention to his company until this bear ends.

62406939
>>62406801
See >>62406897 (wrongly replied to myself).
Also, the real risks are that dividend payments from STR products can be suspended as they see fit, and there is no requirement to protect the principal at any cost.
This is why the death spiral fantasies of Strategy running itself and BTC into the ground are unrealistic. They don't have to do this, so they won't.
However, rugpulling STR products, which they could do in theory, would hurt their credit line, stock price, etc. If you check the STRC holders and say Yahoo Finance you'll see that BlackRock, VanEck and Fidelity ETFs put hundreds of millions into this. Strategy is unlikely to rugpull them.
Realistically they can easily sit out a bear market for multiple years, they'll easily be able to pay the dividends and make some effort to get back to $100 par, but you can't depend on this especially in the near term.

62406959
Where Saylor get the money? There must be some glitch.. he keep buying at loss and pay High interest but he don't produce nothing useful. That's like a life cheat code, the ultimate Ponzi materialization

62406974
>>62406959
he just needs to increase the strc yield to 35%

62407037
>>62406934
>His bitcoin holdings are 50b now, at the money, we are expecting bitcoin to drop at least 30% further and hit 40k, so that will get closer and closer to his debt. From experience and seeing what normies are saying, they are also claiming 40k so I expect going even lower.
The price of STRC is controlled using the open market.
Strategy sold each STRC for $100 and bought $100 worth of BTC at (I think) an average of something like $75k.
Strategy committed to paying $11.5 per share per year (likely going up a bit in the future to try and restore the par value).
If they are able to sell the BTC they purchased using the $100 at their purchase price, they have 9 years of dividend coverage just from selling the BTC bought using STRC.
If the BTC they purchased falls 50% when they sell, they still have 4.5 years of dividend coverage from selling that BTC alone - ignoring all of their other holdings, ignoring common stock dilution, and ignoring other credit market options.
Notably, Strategy is not required to buy back any shares and give you back the $100 directly. If you want your $100 back, you have to sell into the open market. At this time only dividend, which they did commit to paying (but which they can suspend at any time) is used to try and keep the shares at $100. I think in the future they are likely to buy back to stabilize the price, but there is no requirement to death-spiral into anything.

62407111
>>62406974
Looks like a fraud to me

62407163
Bitcoin is getting Stretched!

62407588
I have a feeling MSTR is in the same situation that FTX was. Their books are all good, they have strong investments, but they have just enough weird shit going on that they can't survive a bank run in the middle of a bear market.

62407749
>>62406959
Yes. Thats the problem. This retard thought everyone would switch to Bitcoin so it would be fine but it doesnt look like it.

62407785
>>62407588
>Their books are all good
FTX's books weren't all good. They had a giant hole because the weasel thought stoplosses were for pussies, so Alameda lost billions, and SBF funneled customers money from FTX to Alameda to try to recover the losses

62407826
>>62407785
what i mean is, FTX would have been fine/profitable in a normal bull or bear market. Their mistake was having enough risk that they were unable to survive a bear market + black swan

62407842
>>62407826
nah they were misusing customer deposits. The slightest hint of withdrawal issues would have and did cause them to collapse

62407860
>>62407826
>they would have been fine if the fraud hadn't come to light
Yeah, I mean... So would Bernie Madoff

62407942
>>62405528
>and literally in a Luna
hello saar

62408624
>>62407826
strategy is orders of magnitude better at risk management than FTX. Look at their debt ratio

62408768
>>62408624
There's also a difference between running a private company out of the Bahamas and a multi-billion public company in the US.
For Strategy the main question is whether they hold the claimed coins, and for this not to be the case requires an unlikely combination of a accounting/finance/controlling scam with the execs and/or auditor and/or custodians looking the other way.
Things like the Enron scam can happen, but this was a rare event which wiped out the auditor that colluded with them and turned the Big 5 into the Big 4 of accounting.
Arkham supposedly tracked down some of their holdings as well.

62409189
>>62406974
Anything above 20% would make it pointless to even sell strc and it would be easier if they rebuy it back.

62409206
>>62406788
Now that I think about it, they are seriously incentivised to stop increasing the dividend and to just keep it at that level while waiting for people to sell at a large loss so they can buy back at a discount.

62409599
>>62405528
100%.

62409770
>>62409206
>>62409206
An incentive does exist, but there's also an incentive to watch out for those shareholders so they are able to sell more shares in the future. Looking at it as an incentive frames it, as usual, as a "Strategy trying to rip everyone off" thing. If you were to give them the benefit of of the doubt the main take-away might just be that it's not the end of the world.

The current sentiment is that everything is a collapse scenario. But STRC trading at a discount is not a collapse because it might enable them to buy back debt cheaply. They recently also bought back 1.5b in convertible notes for 1.3b from their cash reserve because those notes had a 0% coupon and the conversion became unrealistic, so holders of those notes agreed to sell them back at the discount. That's where the whole "STRC depegging" thing really kicked off because they ate into the cash reserve, but there are many things to like about reducing debt and doing so at a discount.

Another way to look at STRC outside of the endless "ponzi" accusations is they they bet on dollar depreciation over the long term here (not an unreasonable bet in general):
- They collect $100 for the promise to pay back $11.5 per year, giving them 9 years of runway.
- These $100 are used to buy BTC.
- If BTC goes up (preferably sooner than later) in the next 9 years
There are many scenarios where they can make out like bandits without ripping anyone off. How about:
- There's a 2y bear market where they pay $23 for every $100 share in dividends
- Trust is never fully restored, there are no new buyers
- BTC eventually goes up again and doubles back to its ATH
- They sell half of the STRC-financed BTC, pay back STRC fully, and pocket the difference minus paid dividends (23%)

Everyone got paid in dividends and received the principal back, and Strategy still made a profit for the company.

62409999
>>62405528

Saylor promoted $STR to risk-averse retirees by assuring them that all the volatility had been stripped out. STRC is down over 5% today, more than 17% below what many retirees paid last month. Almost two years of dividends gone.
@Saylor clearly made material misrepresentations.

It's true that I never bought into the Bitcoin narrative, but I always respected the views of those who disagreed with me and their right to express their opinions. But I drew a line with
@Saylor defrauding investors with his
$STRC Ponzi scheme and those who covered for him.

So far the low on $STRC is $80. That's a 20% drop in an investment @Saylor promoted as safe, with near-zero volatility. At that price, the current yield for new buyers is 14.4%. Clearly, investors don't believe the dividend is sustainable and expect it to be reduced or suspended.

62412142
>>62409999
>retirees
Watching boomers get fucked So Saylor can buy more Bitcoin is fine by me. I hope they went all in on SpaceX too.

item.title: They would never make a pump and dump with the whole US stock market, right? — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
They would never make a pump and dump with the whole US stock market, right?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-25 18:20 UTC

OP

They would never make a pump and dump with the whole US stock market, right?

Early replies (first 3)

62410863
zoom -ack

62410865
The infamous Bart Simpson

62410871
>>62410856
Cz, is that u?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62410903
>>62410856
that was me, I needed a new jet

62410925
Still up

62410994
>>62410925
Hey it’s the guy who profited off making everything suck who probably made this money programming the shit that made everything suck. What do you want to say to him?

62411123
>>62410994
Kek, guilty

62411186
El barto

62411199
>>62410994
If anyone else wrote this it would get a dozen "you have to be 18+ to post here" (you)s. Whats your secret?

62411220
>>62411199
Being rightbyou streetshitting subhuman

62411757
>>62410856
Here's a redpill for the ages: the entire stock market is just a long waveform pump and dump consisting of many small waveform pump and dumps

62412294
kill all megarich

item.title: Oil is an easy long here. Price has fallen to almost pre-war levels, for literally no reason because the strait is still… — 14 replies — 44.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Oil is an easy long here. Price has fallen to almost pre-war levels, for literally no reason because the strait is still…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-25 18:03 UTC

OP

Oil is an easy long here. Price has fallen to almost pre-war levels, for literally no reason because the strait is still largely closed and reserves are still being drained at unprecedented rates. The Strategic Petroleum Reserve in the US has lost 20% of its oil, nearly 100 million barrels, in just two months, and it's not slowing down. Even if the strait somehow were to open immediately and fully, production and transport will take months or even years to normalize. We should be rationing but the government is too scared to do so, they must keep up appearances. This is the recipe for an enormous squeeze.

Early replies (first 3)

62407877
>>62407727
You’re poor.

62407971
>>62407727
>>62407949
Kys you stupid begger >>62407877
Basedo ik from Argentina my family struggles a lot but I hate poors

62407975
>>62407558
I've been getting decent returns from investing in Caterpillar Inc, so I think I'll ride that out.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408056
>>62407558
You should stop getting your news from turd worlders.

62408101
>giga crowded normal fag take

We are entering oversupply

62408144
>>62407558
They can slow panic for another 2-3 weeks but its already showing up in every other asset class. The cure to high prices is high prices, the cure to low prices is low prices. This is the only real golden rule for me anymore when it comes to commodities. If prices are held artificially lower to say spare the bond market briefly then the reaction to mean is all the more violent. Always invert, and the problem is clear as day.

62409475
>>62407558
You didn't hear? Trump said the strait is open

62409516
>>62407558
I'm long oil but some bearish arguments are that Venezuela and Iran are now no longer sanctioned, at least 60 days for Iran. China still on a buyers strike, unknown when they'll start buying. Tankers were/are getting through with the transponders turned off. Hits on Russian refineries might make Russia sell it's surplus putting downward pressure on the price.

62409526
>>62409516
>unknown when they'll start buying

Where do you think all those Iranian tankers are going?

62409537
>>62407558
>As of the week ending June 19, 2026, the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) holds 331.19 million barrels of crude oil, putting it at its lowest level in over four decades

>The physical safety threshold where the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) caverns face severe structural risk and potential collapse is estimated to be between 150 and 160 million barrels.

>243 to 245 Million Barrels (The Policy Floor): The absolute operational limit currently committed under recent strategic drawdowns. Going past this threshold enters unchartered territory for cavern physics

>150 to 160 Million Barrels (The Physical Collapse Threshold): This is the physical "cushion" line analyzed by institutions like JPMorgan. Dropping below this point leaves the caverns without the internal fluid pressure needed to counteract the immense weight of the surrounding salt domes, risking catastrophic collapse.

>While the physical collapse line is roughly 171 million barrels away, the U.S. will likely stop drawing oil well before then. Dropping below 300 million barrels starves the system of hydrostatic pressure. This slows maximum emergency pumping rates by up to 30%, which mathematically cripples the reserve's usefulness during a crisis long before a cavern physically implodes.

62409539
>>62407558
>The Strategic Petroleum Reserve in the US has lost 20% of its oil, nearly 100 million barrels, in just two months, and it's not slowing down. Even if the strait somehow were to open immediately and fully, production and transport will take months or even years to normalize. We should be rationing
The US is the world's largest oil and natural gas producer.
We're not going to "run out" of either, even if it becomes more expensive.
>but a lot of what's refined here is actually imported--
yeah, from Canada, Mexico, and now Venezuela.
And if prices go up enough, dormant shale basin sources (which got wiped out during COVID) will be developed again.
>but investors will be to skittish to reinvest in shale players given much money they lost--
Do you not understand energy investors?

62409543
>>62409539
https://www.eia.gov/tools/faqs/faq.php?id=727&t=6

62409548
>>62409543
>US energy imports.

62412226
>>62409539
>The US is the world's largest oil and natural gas producer.
>We're not going to "run out" of either, even if it becomes more expensive.
Then why are they needing to draw from the SPR at this extremely fast rate? At this rate it WILL run out. Yes, it's entirely possible to avoid that by slowing down consumption to levels that match the production. And that's exactly what I mean by rationing. But they're not doing that yet. And once they do (because they will have to), price will skyrocket, hence my long.

item.title: >diversify into BTC, ETH, gold >they pulled me down so hard that it wiped out all my stock gains since jan '25 And I tho… — 14 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>diversify into BTC, ETH, gold >they pulled me down so hard that it wiped out all my stock gains since jan '25 And I tho…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-25 17:56 UTC

OP

>diversify into BTC, ETH, gold
>they pulled me down so hard that it wiped out all my stock gains since jan '25
And I thought I was smart for loading up at 70k after it fell from ATH

Early replies (first 3)

62407385
>>62407300
>diversify from high risk assets by buying giga high risk assets
???

62407437
>>62407300
you should sell all your crypto right now and then rebuy it all asap. the tax man cometh.

62408183
The cold hard truth is NOTHING is a good buy right now. MAYBE housing is, but literally nothing else. Crypto has zero hype ever returning to it (unlike the '22 crash which still had some hopeful bagholders hanging around), stocks are near bubble burst, precious metals turned into another pnd scheme, businesses have outrageously expensive overhead, etc.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408190
>>62407300
>fell for it again award

62408207
>>62408183
High interest accounts waiting to scoop up cheap deals after the bubble burst is the play

62408211
>>62408183
Cash is king. I only figured that out in May though :/

62409836
>>62407385
Gold isn't giga high risk. BTC and ETH will never hit 0 and both can be staked.
>>62408211
I have over 30k in cash with 4.5% yield, waiting to slurp. It feels like the bottom is nowhere near.

62409937
>>62409836
>facts proved him wrong
>explain him why he was wrong
>still doesn’t get it

62409943
>>62407300
>investment horizon of one year
when will you impulsive niggers ever learn

62409945
>>62408183
Stocks are fine, retard. There will probably be a pullback in the next 1-2 years, but it'll be like 10-15%, and it'll recover in 18 months. It will have precisely 0 effect on anyone investing long term.

You crypto farm animals deal with the most absurd volatility and risk of ruin the universe has ever seen. You deserve death simply because you are incapable of hearing the reasoning people have been shouting at you for years.

62409949
>>62409945
>tfw Anon has a crystal ball that actually works, and we don’t

62409954
>>62409949
Well, you can either bet on the one thing (stocks) that have survived many "catastrophes" and continued trucking along, because they are pinned to the productive output of 8 billion people. OR, you can invest in fake internet money that is pinned to nothing in the real world. Just the dreams of NEETs.

62411889
>>62407300
You only lose when you sell, DCA and keep holding. I bought around 65K too and I staked it, the pump will come again

62412198
>>62408183
>Crypto is dead, FOREVER THIS TIME
yeah it's going to be Ethereum

item.title: What the actual FUCK is this? — 13 replies — 24.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What the actual FUCK is this?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-25 17:50 UTC

OP

What the actual FUCK is this?

Early replies (first 3)

62410973
>>62410915
Thank you for playing

62411210
They just did their AMA but banned any questions related to:

- The token
- Revenue
- Unlocks
- Buy backs
- The reserve
- Staking
- Timelines

They basically just responded to a bunch of questions like "We know Chainlink is the industry standard and already being adopted by major institutions but how can the community help"
Follow by answers like
"Talk to your friends and family to spread awareness, follow us on social media and share our posts"

It was just an excuse for them to ask holders to shill LINK to their family so they can get more liquidity to dump the 18 million tokens that unlocked for them this week

62411645
>>62411210
Buy our tokens so we can keep making these 'patnership' headlines that you read on secondary market (4chan)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62411772
>>62410915
A visual representation of how Chainlink Labs is in fact ABOVE the chainlink TOKEN.
The link token will continue its downward trajectory into irrelevance

62411846
>>62411210
brutal.

t. i own 565 links tokwens

62411852
>>62411645
jesus fuking christ

62411855
>>62410973
i really did miss another bullrun didnt i .


i promise to god im selling it all next bull run when we hit 17 dollars, cant sell now... too late for that.

62412054
>>62411855
TNN

You really fell for a bunch of buzzwords and low tier shilling by schizophrenic baggies kek

62412075
>>62410915
Aaaaaaah it's over!!
I should have listened to the fuddies! I should have sold and bought xrp instead! Aaaaaaaaa!

62412108
>>62412054
yup i wasted 4 years of my life invesnting to get like 600 link tokens

62412114
>>62412075
And here we see a link marine being unable to comprehend that he and the xrp army are two faces of the same retarded coin.

62412123
>>62412114
Nooooo! I should have bought BTC! AAAAAAAAAA

62412174
>>62412123

item.title: put most of my life savings into BTC near the top (please don't ask why I didn't DCA). — 16 replies — 10.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
put most of my life savings into BTC near the top (please don't ask why I didn't DCA).

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-25 16:46 UTC

OP

put most of my life savings into BTC near the top (please don't ask why I didn't DCA). i'm really hurting bros. i need to cash out bad in the next few months but i keep literally praying to God for a reversal.

Early replies (first 3)

62410297
>>62410269
Kneepads, faggg

62410303
You didn't lose anything unless you sold

62410306
The concept of speculation is demonic. God will not be answering this prayer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62410312
>>62410297
huh?

>>62410303
not yet but I'm really hurting for liquidity right now. if i cashed out right now i lock in 180k loss..

>>62410306
why demonic

62410318
no reversal until 30-40K

62410335
>>62410269
I hope you are in it for the tech (cyber hornets).

62410337
>>62410312
What do you need so much money for? Also never invest money you arent prepared to lose.

62410340
>>62410269
Be happy, be absolutely manic, that you didn't put it into some altcoin instead.
Cut your losses, sell, and get out.

62410402
>>62410269
>i need to cash out bad
This is why you never invest money you aren't willing to completely lose. No crying in the casino, chud. You did this to yourself.

62410406
It fell only like 50% from the top, though? That can happen to anything.

62410434
>>62410406
The likelihood increases with the volatility of the asset. Fast gains and fast losses are the exact same thing, the only difference is which side of the transaction got the fast gain and which side got the fast loss.

62410437
>>62410269
If you need to sell it's okay. Let this be a lesson to you. You only buy bitcoin with a minimum 5 year time horizon. Anything less and you're almost guaranteed to have a bad time. At least you can lock in the capital loss and carry it forward.

62410438
>>62410312
you only need the rate of inflation +1-2% for posterity
anything more is heretical

62410460
>>62410269
put my life savings in altcoin rugs at 2021, life ruined, so it could be worse for you

cheer up

62411803
>>62410303
Exactly instead of selling if you need liquidity just lend it, I know custody issues is a thing, I really hope TBV comes live soon

62411859
>>62410269
HAHAHA NO REFUNDS, THANKS FOR PLAYING, MY LITTLE EXIT LIQUIDITY

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 157 replies — 66.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 157 • Updated: 2026-06-25 15:59 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398813
There, lets read over these three

62398868
>>62398799
You are better off getting a job

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

item.title: BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!! — 50 replies — 27.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-25 16:33 UTC

OP

>Today, Chainlink, FairSquareLab, a first-mover in core infrastructure within the Korean digital asset ecosystem, UniKA (Unified Korea Alliance), which represents the Korean coalition behind Project Pangea and is comprised of a steering committee of 5 entities (Shinhan Bank, JB Bank, Kbank, FairSquareLab, and OBDIA) along with 10+ participating Korean commercial banks, and Qivalis, a euro stablecoin consortium powered by 37 leading European banks, announced Project Pangea, a strategic, collaborative task force focused on redefining global foreign exchange markets by unlocking real-time, stablecoin-based cross-border settlement models.


https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/chainlink-and-multinational-banking-consortia-launch-project-pangea-to-develop-t0-settlement-framework-for-international-fx-markets-302807910.html

Early replies (first 3)

62401710
Wow I can’t wait for this to not mean anything.

62401722
p+c etc.
don't care until the needle moves on this FUCKING GODDAMN TOKEN

62401738
>>62401704
xrp sneeded?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62407916
the chainlink llm must be well trained by now. the loop of
>generate buzzword headline
>gauge token buying response
>reinforce learning
has been going on for years, it's getting very effective

62408509
>>62403111
Trips of seething

62408891
>>62402814
>4 things
>4chan
illuminati confirmed

62409583
>>62406112
But Chino-chan, why would the price of Bitcoin have anything to do with the price of Chainlink?

62409593
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SHAKALA. I am new to 4chan but have been loving this meme lately! I read on reddit yesterday that this would happen and I boughted (but unfortunately the price basically immediately soon after). I was still so pumped I went to Costco and got a slice of pizza and sat facing those walking out looking at everyone walking by knowing and rye-ly smiling knowing none of them understood what is happening. I felt like Brownstone Capital with Ben Rickerts in the Big Short. I had to stop myself from celebrating because I remembered they were missing out and I wanted to feel the same type of compassion they were reminded to feel by Ben in that moment so I finished my pizza solemnly leaving a bite out of clenched stomach respect for them not being able to finish my meal, grabbed my bag, put my hood up, and walked out nodding to the door inspector knowing looking at them knowingly and humanly as I walked out with them smiling not knowing beneath my shades how I was really seeing them. It was an amazing experience and it felt like everyone was watching but I know they weren't because the thing that made it special was that they don't understand or see. I will come back and tip the kitchen staff handsomely (especially the girl who I like but have not had the courage to ask out....yet) when we are making bookoo bucks and I can afford to own the whole store and promote her. and then smirk as I tell her I will sell the store, non-chalantly, so that I can ask her so she would feel comfortable if I ask her out on a date after I made her general manager of the store. I can't wait!

62409606
>>62409593
This might literally be the gayest, most link investor typified thing I have ever seen on this site in over a decade of lurking and posting all day and it is way too perfect to be a troll. Holy fucking shit.

62409613
>62409593
>62409606
Obvious samefag, absolutely pathetic lol

62409630
>>62409613
What does that mean (seems like a slur, no)? I just wanted to share a really awesome moment where I had a sort of micro-epiphany and specific sort of sonder imagining the future and knowing what we know, how most people just don't see. Why is this a bad thing? It was the best day I have had in a long time and I have been feeling really optimistic ever since and the awe I felt in this moment carried over really perfectly into the movie I went to see after (Disclosure Day), which had me thinking about how even though we see so much and are so knowledgeable, imagine what the aliens must see! There is probably some sort of alien Chainlink that other aliens zip around feeling this way about how we don't see or or maybe even a super Chainlink that they know that other aliens don't, if they aren't a hivemind super-intelligence or transcendent above the type of feeling or incapable of empathy at all. I just had a really deep moment and layed in bed thinking about it as well as how my revelation carried into the movie and then up up and away and I wanted to share it hoping it made someone else here think deep thoughts or feel optimistic too! We are going to be so freaking rich!!!

62409633
>what does that mean?
fucking newfags

62409639
>>62409633
Why do you guys keep calling me gay? I mean, I am bi but what's wrong with that? I am one of you! We should be able to band together Voltron this shit! LINK doesn't need us to for it to thrive but we might as well be in harmony as our investment carries us hand in hand to the moon!

62409640
>>62409606
>>62409613

62409661
>>62409640
How i feel in this thread! Meanwhile, I got the picture I am attaching to this comment in my back pocket, (which none of them know) just waiting to pull it out like the ultimate trump card. (¬‿ ͡¬) Dare I?

62409666
>>62406112
I don't know, but I sipped more link

62409669
>>62409666
>>62409583

62409675
>mB3FqHQS

62409679
>>62409675
Who is this? He looks crazy and scared. Is this from a movie or something? I don't recognize it. TL;DR?

62409681
>>62409675
I just saw you named the picture xrp. Are you saying this is XRP realizing the severity of what I posted in "62409661?" If so, post it again, because you are surely right!

62409739
i don't care anymore. they could do away with the token and send it to 0 and I would just post a LINK and picrel crossover thread to laugh at the copers who will be forever employed and get on with the rest of my life as if nothing happened and i didn't spend thkusands of hours reseaeching bullshit and making rarely reposted link memes for 7 years.
fuck you saturn

62409752
>>62409661
bless the undeserving, king

62409768
>>62409739
do things like that really happen though? No watchers, no justice? the whole token just poof?

62409835
>>62409752
*drops shades revealing the same knowing look from before, but sharper, and slams the picture down on table* BOOOOOM!

62409844
>>62409835

62409848
>>62409844
Exactly!

62409884
>>62409768
the token doesn't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes, you can incentivize everyone with other assets, staking has obviously been unnecessary to secure value etc. it's "happened" in all but name. but it might work out anyway, idk.

62409907
price

62410035
>>62409884
>the token doesn't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes
Same is true for Bitcoin, ETH, ...
Bitcoin was arguably way more secure when it was literal cents, and random faggots ran full mining nodes on their home PCs.

62410246
>mB3FqHQS
I've said it before and I'll say it again: the BOOOOOM meme absolutely destroyed some xerpies on here. Can't believe that after all the abuse and bullying they took for 10 years, simply posting the catchphrase of one of their twitter schizos is what did them in. Praying for you crazy bastards.

62411245
>>62410035
>duuuude it's not fair, bitcoin did xyz...

so how does it work in your head? 40 million altcoins should all have bitcoin or ethereum type price runup so things can become... fair?

you see folks, this is how the logic of a chainlink boyscout operates. no understanding that there isn't enough liquidity for their special altcoin to run like early crypto's did back when there was only like 50 of them. no understanding that obviously they aren't early anymore and therefore the days of those speculative runs are over.

instead of accepting the market they have, they cry like a child complaining to their parents that the other kids were allowed to have cookies before dinner.

they are: the few, the retarded... THE Cuckolds of crypto

>>62410035
i don't think the same is true for btc and eth. their ongoing function and adoption (by miners) depended on value realizable by the token. Link apparently doesn't have the same restriction.
